tests.scenario: Free Summary Report After Initial Assessment Completion
steps: Create a new user account or use a user with no completed assessment
Complete the full initial assessment period required by the app
Finish the final assessment check-in without starting a subscription
tests.expected_result: The system displays at least one viewable summary report without requiring payment.

tests.scenario: Free User Can Open Generated Initial Summary Report
steps: Use a free user account that has already completed the initial assessment period
Navigate to the reports or summary section
Open the generated initial summary report
tests.expected_result: The initial summary report opens successfully and is viewable without a subscription purchase.

tests.scenario: No Payment Prompt Required to View First Assessment Summary
steps: Use a free user account that has just completed the initial assessment period
Attempt to access the first generated report immediately after completion
Observe whether a paywall or purchase prompt blocks access
tests.expected_result: The user can view the first generated summary report without being blocked by a payment requirement.

tests.scenario: Pricing Disclosure Before Initial Assessment Begins
steps: Install and open the app as a new user
Proceed to the point immediately before starting the initial assessment
Review the pricing or feature disclosure screen
tests.expected_result: The screen lists free features, paid features, subscription price, billing period, and renewal status before the user proceeds.

tests.scenario: Pricing Disclosure Before Premium Trial Starts
steps: Open the app with a user who has not started a premium trial
Select the option to start a premium trial
Review the information shown before confirming the trial
tests.expected_result: The system discloses which features are free, which require payment, the price, billing period, and whether billing renews automatically before the trial starts.

tests.scenario: Renewal Status Visible on Pricing Screen
steps: Navigate to a premium purchase or trial start screen
Inspect the subscription details shown before acceptance
Look for auto-renewal information
tests.expected_result: The pricing screen explicitly states whether billing renews automatically.

tests.scenario: Existing Reports Remain Viewable After Subscription Cancellation
steps: Use a subscribed account with previously generated reports
Cancel the subscription
Navigate to the reports section after cancellation
tests.expected_result: Previously generated reports remain viewable after subscription cancellation.

tests.scenario: Existing Journal Entries Remain Accessible After Subscription Expiry
steps: Use an account with saved journal entries and an active subscription
Allow the subscription to expire
Open the journal history section
tests.expected_result: Previously saved journal entries remain accessible after subscription expiry.

tests.scenario: Existing History Remains Available Without Renewing Subscription
steps: Use an account with prior mood history, reports, and saved entries
End or cancel the subscription without renewing
Access the history section
tests.expected_result: The user can still view previously generated reports, history, and journal entries without renewing the subscription.

tests.scenario: Successful Cold Launch to Interactive Screen
steps: Install the app on a supported device with working internet connectivity
Launch the app from a closed state
Wait for startup to complete
tests.expected_result: The app opens to an interactive dashboard or questionnaire screen without crashing or showing a blank screen.

tests.scenario: Launch From Notification Opens Functional Questionnaire Screen
steps: Use a supported device with working connectivity and an active app notification
Tap the app notification
Observe the screen that opens
tests.expected_result: The app opens to a functional interactive screen and does not display a blank screen or crash.

tests.scenario: Relaunch After Force Close Remains Stable
steps: Open the app on a supported device with working connectivity
Force close the app
Launch the app again
tests.expected_result: The app relaunches to an interactive dashboard or questionnaire screen without crashing or showing a blank screen.

tests.scenario: Valid Sign-In Loads Saved History
steps: Open the app sign-in screen
Enter valid account credentials
Submit the sign-in request
tests.expected_result: The user is authenticated successfully and their saved history is displayed.

tests.scenario: Valid Sign-In After Reinstall Loads Same History
steps: Use an account with saved history
Reinstall the app
Sign in with valid credentials
tests.expected_result: The user is authenticated successfully and the previously saved history is loaded.

tests.scenario: Returning User Sign-In Opens Account History
steps: Log out of an account that has existing saved history
Return to the sign-in screen
Sign back in with valid credentials
tests.expected_result: The returning user is authenticated and their account history becomes available after sign-in.

tests.scenario: Entries Persist After App Update
steps: Use the app to create and save multiple entries
Update the application to a newer version
Open the entries history after the update
tests.expected_result: All previously saved entries remain accessible after the update.

tests.scenario: Reports Persist After App Update
steps: Generate at least one report in the current app version
Update the application
Navigate to the reports section
tests.expected_result: Previously generated reports remain accessible after the application update.

tests.scenario: Updated App Retains Existing User Data on Reopen
steps: Use an account with saved entries and reports
Update the app and reopen it
Check both the history and reports sections
tests.expected_result: The updated app retains access to all previously saved entries and reports.

tests.scenario: Optional Data Categories Are Disclosed Before Consent
steps: Proceed to a screen requesting account, health, or device-sharing data beyond core check-ins
Read the information displayed for each requested optional data category
Observe the consent controls
tests.expected_result: The system lists each optional data category, explains its purpose, and requests explicit user consent before collection.

tests.scenario: User Can Decline Optional Data Sharing
steps: Open a data collection screen for optional categories
Decline consent for at least one optional data category
Continue using core check-in features
tests.expected_result: The optional data is not collected and the user can continue using core check-in features.

tests.scenario: Explicit Consent Required for Device-Sharing Data
steps: Navigate to a screen that requests device-sharing or analytics data
Review the disclosure shown
Attempt to proceed without actively granting consent
tests.expected_result: The system does not treat device-sharing consent as granted unless the user actively provides consent.

tests.scenario: Account Creation With Valid Required Fields
steps: Open the account creation screen
Enter valid values in all required fields
Submit the account creation form
tests.expected_result: The account is created successfully.

tests.scenario: Entries Are Linked to Newly Created Account
steps: Create a new account using valid required fields
Complete and save a new entry after account creation
Open the account history
tests.expected_result: The saved entry is stored under the newly created account.

tests.scenario: Second Entry Is Stored Under Same Account
steps: Create a new account successfully
Save two separate entries while signed in
Review the account history
tests.expected_result: Both entries are linked to the same created account and appear in that account’s history.

tests.scenario: Restore Synchronized Data After Reinstall on Same Device
steps: Use a signed-in account with previously synchronized entries and reports
Reinstall the application on the same device
Sign back in to the same account
tests.expected_result: The user’s synchronized entries and reports are restored after reinstall.

tests.scenario: Restore Synchronized Data on New Device
steps: Use an account with previously synchronized data
Install the app on a second supported device
Sign in with the same account on the second device
tests.expected_result: The synchronized entries and reports are restored on the new device.

tests.scenario: Restored Data Includes Both Entries and Reports
steps: Use an account with synchronized entries and at least one report
Sign in after reinstall or on another device
Check both the entries history and reports section
tests.expected_result: Both synchronized entries and reports are restored successfully.

tests.scenario: Save Reminder Settings With Frequency and Time Windows
steps: Open reminder settings
Enable reminders and choose a daily frequency and preferred time windows
Save the reminder settings
tests.expected_result: The system stores the selected reminder enablement state, frequency, and preferred time windows.

tests.scenario: Disable Reminders in Settings
steps: Open reminder settings
Turn reminder enablement off
Save the updated settings
tests.expected_result: The system stores reminders as disabled.

tests.scenario: Modify Existing Reminder Preferences
steps: Open reminder settings for a user with existing reminder preferences
Change the daily frequency and time windows
Save the updated preferences
tests.expected_result: The system stores the modified frequency and preferred reminder time windows.

tests.scenario: Reminder Continues After One Missed Check-In
steps: Enable reminders for a user account
Miss one scheduled check-in without disabling reminders
Wait for the next scheduled reminder time
tests.expected_result: The system still issues the next reminder according to the saved schedule.

tests.scenario: Reminder Continues After Multiple Missed Check-Ins
steps: Enable reminders for a user account
Miss multiple scheduled check-ins
Wait for the next scheduled reminder time
tests.expected_result: The system continues issuing reminders according to the saved schedule until the user disables reminders.

tests.scenario: No Reminder Issued After User Disables Reminders
steps: Enable reminders for a user account
Open reminder settings and disable reminders
Wait for the next previously scheduled reminder time
tests.expected_result: The system does not issue a reminder after the user has disabled reminders.

tests.scenario: Employment Context Excludes Inapplicable Work Questions
steps: Open the user context settings
Declare a context factor indicating the user is not employed
Generate the next questionnaire
tests.expected_result: The questionnaire excludes clearly inapplicable work-related questions.

tests.scenario: Disability Context Excludes Inapplicable Health Assumption Questions
steps: Open the user context settings
Declare a disability or chronic illness context factor
Generate the next questionnaire
tests.expected_result: The questionnaire excludes clearly inapplicable questions that conflict with the declared health context.

tests.scenario: Atypical Work Schedule Context Affects Questionnaire Relevance
steps: Open the user context settings
Declare an atypical work schedule
Generate the next questionnaire
tests.expected_result: The generated questionnaire excludes clearly inapplicable questions based on the declared atypical work schedule.

tests.scenario: Same Non-Required Question Not Reused More Than Twice in 7 Days
steps: Use a test account to complete multiple check-ins within a 7-day period
Track the appearance count of a non-frequent question
Continue generating questionnaires within the same rolling 7-day period
tests.expected_result: The same non-frequent question text does not appear more than twice within the rolling 7-day period.

tests.scenario: Required frequent Question May Exceed Reuse Limit
steps: Use a questionnaire configuration that includes a question explicitly marked as required for frequent assessment
Complete multiple check-ins within a 7-day period
Track repeated appearances of the marked question
tests.expected_result: A question explicitly marked as required for frequent assessment may appear more than twice within the rolling 7-day period.

tests.scenario: Question Reuse Allowed Again After Rolling 7-Day Window Passes
steps: Use a test account to receive the same non-frequent question twice within 7 days
Wait until the earlier appearances fall outside the rolling 7-day window
Generate a new questionnaire
tests.expected_result: The previously limited non-frequent question may appear again once the rolling 7-day window has passed.

tests.scenario: Generated Report Shows Pattern Summary and Date Range
steps: Use a user account with enough completed entries for report generation
Generate a report
Review the displayed report content
tests.expected_result: The report displays at least one summary of recent user-entered patterns and the date range covered by the report.

tests.scenario: Previously Generated Report Remains Viewable
steps: Generate a report for a user account with sufficient entries
Navigate away from the report screen
Return to the reports history and reopen the report
tests.expected_result: The generated report remains viewable and still shows pattern summary information and the covered date range.

tests.scenario: Report Date Range Matches Input Period
steps: Create enough entries over a known date interval to generate a report
Generate the report
Compare the displayed report date range to the known entry interval used
tests.expected_result: The report identifies the date range covered by the report and that range matches the underlying reporting period.

tests.scenario: Export Generated Report Successfully
steps: Use an account with an existing generated report
Open the report
Select the export action
tests.expected_result: The system produces an exportable report file.

tests.scenario: Exported Report Contains Report Content and Date Range
steps: Generate or open an existing report
Export the report
Open the exported file
tests.expected_result: The exported report file contains the report content and the report date range.

tests.scenario: User Can Export Report Intended for Healthcare Sharing
steps: Use an account with a generated report
Select the export option from the report screen
Save the exportable file to the device
tests.expected_result: The system creates a report file that can be saved and shared with a healthcare professional.
